title: lupinus microcarpus var. ruber
intro: Lupinus microcarpus var. ruber, also known as the Red Field Lupine, is a variety of the annual or perennial herb Lupinus microcarpus, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant contains a high concentration of alkaloids, specifically lupanine and sparteine, which exhibit medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Lupinus microcarpus var. ruber include its use in traditional medicine to treat conditions like digestive issues, respiratory problems, and menstrual cramps. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
